headline: AI's hitting a wall—companies aren't scaling past pilots and the real work is just starting

stories.title: The 'Last Mile' Problem Slowing AI Transformation
stories.summary: Companies are stuck between pilots and real scale. The actual obstacle isn't bad models or data—it's the organizational mess: process debt, tribal knowledge silos, and nobody knows how to govern AI agents.
stories.takeaway: Transformation fails when you treat it as tech problem instead of redesigning how work actually happens across the org
stories.strategyTake: Clean-sheet process redesign > bolt-on AI tools. Agentic governance is non-negotiable now.

stories.title: Publicis Sapient CEO on Why AI Isn't Just About Speed—It's About Strategy
stories.summary: Proof-of-concepts don't scale unless you rethink *how* your business actually works. Real strategic AI moves (like cutting car design time from 18 months to 18 weeks) ripple through supply chain, pricing, and market pivots.
stories.takeaway: AI success isn't functional optimization—it's forcing orgs to operate faster and smarter across the entire system
stories.strategyTake: AI forces you to rethink strategy tempo. Annual cycles & budgets don't work anymore. Speed = competitive advantage.

stories.title: McKinsey Global Tech Agenda 2026: CIOs Are Now Strategy Architects
stories.summary: Top CIOs are rewriting their companies around agentic AI—embedding tech into business model innovation, not just operations. Nearly 1/3 of top performers prioritizing tech-led business model redesign over next 2 years.
stories.takeaway: The old tech/business separation is dead. CIOs who don't sit at strategy table will get left behind hard
stories.company: DBS Bank (case example of product-platform model)
stories.strategyTake: Top companies investing 10%+ more on tech budgets. Scale agentic AI or get crushed by peers doing it.

stories.title: The Emerging Agentic Enterprise: Managing AI as Both Tool AND Coworker
stories.summary: 76% of execs see agentic AI as a coworker, not just a tool. But orgs are treating it like tech, not talent—so governance, roles, and accountability frameworks are all breaking down.
stories.takeaway: Agencies are forcing the biggest org design challenge in decades. Tech and strategy teams *must* merge or fail.
stories.source: MIT Sloan Management Review x BCG Henderson Institute
stories.strategyTake: Single AI agent demands both HR (people) and asset management (tech) approaches—traditional management logic breaks

watchNext.title: To Thrive in the AI Era, Companies Need Agent Managers
watchNext.why: New leadership role emerging—agent manager at Salesforce shows how real companies are structuring AI team oversight & governance as agents go from pilot to production

watchNext.title: Context Engineering as Competitive Edge When Everyone's Using Same AI Models
watchNext.why: When all companies have access to same models, org context (how you actually work, not how you say you work) becomes the differentiator—explains why process redesign > model choice
